steps.title: Introduce the examples
steps.content: The sentence says the transit method has detected exoplanets at vast distances from Earth, then gives a list of examples. A colon after Earth is needed to introduce that list clearly.

steps.title: Place the colon correctly
steps.content: Choice B attaches at 41 light-years away to Earth and puts the colon too late. Choice C uses commas instead of introducing the list. Choice D runs from Earth into the first example without a clear list marker. The remaining choice places the colon after Earth and keeps the first distance with the first exoplanet. The answer is A.
